question.text: Booking profits periodically from growth assets in Bucket 3 and moving them to Bucket 2 is recommended when:

answer.text: Returns exceed a predetermined target, such as 12% per annum
Bucket 3 contains only 10% of the total corpus

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Setting a target return (e.g., 12% p.a.) and booking profits when exceeded helps protect gains and systematically move funds from riskier to safer buckets. This is a disciplined approach to managing growth assets. **The Trap:** Market highs (Option B) do not necessarily mean the target has been reached. Age-based (Option C) and size-based (Option D) triggers are less effective than return-based triggers. **Cross-Exam Context:** Profit booking in retirement portfolios is covered in Series XVII. This connects to portfolio rebalancing concepts in Series V-A and X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The drawback of profit booking is losing compounding benefits. In retirement, capital preservation often takes priority over maximizing compounding — so moderate profit booking is prudent.
